# Flutter BLoC Advance Template

> A production-ready, open-source Flutter starter template using BLoC pattern for state management with Feature-First Clean Architecture. Supports Android, iOS, Web, macOS, Linux, and Windows. Built with Flutter 3.41.4, Dart 3.11.1, flutter_bloc 9.1.1, and go_router 17.1.0.

This project provides a complete foundation for building enterprise-grade Flutter applications. It includes authentication (login, register, forgot password, OTP verification), user management (CRUD operations), role-based access control (Admin/User roles), a responsive design system, dark/light theming, English/Turkish localization, and multi-environment support (local mock data and production API).

## Architecture

Source: https://github.com/cevheri/flutter-bloc-advanced/blob/main/docs/feature-first-clean-boundaries.md

### Overview

The project follows Feature-First Clean Boundaries architecture. The goal is to keep the codebase feature-oriented, make dependency direction explicit and enforceable, preserve the Flutter stack (flutter_bloc, go_router, manual JSON models), and support incremental migration.

### Top-Level Ownership

```
lib/
  app/               # Composition root, app shell, router, bootstrap
  core/              # Cross-cutting primitives, errors, logging abstractions
  infrastructure/    # Storage, HTTP, environment, external adapters
  shared/            # Business-free reusable UI and helpers
  features/          # Business capabilities
  generated/         # Generated localization files
  l10n/              # ARB files
  main/              # Entry points (main_local.dart, main_prod.dart)
```

### Dependency Rules

Allowed imports:

- `app` → features, shared, infrastructure, core
- `features` → shared, infrastructure, core
- `shared` → core only
- `infrastructure` → core only

Forbidden imports:

- `feature_a` → `feature_b/presentation` or `feature_b/data`
- `shared` → `features`
- `core` → `shared` or `features`
- `infrastructure` → `features/presentation`

### Feature Structure

Each feature owns its navigation, state, and data contracts:

```
features/<feature>/
  application/       # BLoC/Cubit + use case orchestration
    <feature>_bloc.dart
    <feature>_event.dart
    <feature>_state.dart
    usecases/
      <action>_usecase.dart
  data/
    models/           # Data models (fromJson/toJson)
    mappers/          # Entity ↔ Model conversions
    repositories/     # Repository implementations
  domain/
    entities/         # Domain entities (Flutter-free)
    repositories/     # Repository interfaces (abstract class)
  navigation/
    <feature>_routes.dart
  presentation/
    pages/            # Screens
    widgets/          # Feature-local widgets
```

### Use Case Pattern

BLoCs depend on use cases, not repositories directly:

```dart
// BLoC → UseCase → Repository Interface
class LoginBloc extends Bloc<LoginEvent, LoginState> {
  LoginBloc({
    required AuthenticateUserUseCase authenticateUserUseCase,
    required SendOtpUseCase sendOtpUseCase,
    required VerifyOtpUseCase verifyOtpUseCase,
    required GetAccountUseCase getAccountUseCase,
  });
}
```

### App Responsibilities

`app/` is the only place that may compose concrete implementations: router composition, global providers, session bootstrap, shell layout, theme and localization bootstrap. It must not contain feature business rules.

### Shared vs Core

Use `shared/` for business-free reusable UI: design system components, dialogs, generic widgets, generic UI helpers. Use `core/` only for primitives that should not depend on Flutter UI concerns: error types, result wrappers, logging contracts, small common types.

### State Management Rules

- UI should never instantiate repositories directly
- Feature pages depend on feature application state only
- Session/auth state is app-level
- Shell state is app-level
- Feature state remains inside the feature module
- BLoCs use Equatable for state equality, copyWith() for immutable updates
- States use status enums: initial, loading, success, failure

### Routing Rules

- `app/router` owns route composition
- Each feature exposes its own routes from `features/<feature>/navigation/`
- Redirect functions must stay pure and free of data loading side effects
- Route builders must not create repositories directly

## Features

### Account (14 files)

- `application/`: AccountBloc + use cases for profile management
- `data/models/`: ChangePassword model
- `data/repositories/`: AccountRepository implementation
- `domain/repositories/`: IAccountRepository interface
- `navigation/`: AccountFeatureRoutes
- `presentation/pages/`: AccountPage

### Auth (30 files)

- `application/`: LoginBloc, RegisterBloc, ForgotPasswordBloc, ChangePasswordBloc + use cases
- `data/models/`: JWTToken, UserJwt, SendOtpRequest, VerifyOtpRequest
- `data/mappers/`: AuthMapper
- `data/repositories/`: LoginRepository (AuthRepositoryImpl)
- `domain/entities/`: AuthEntity
- `domain/repositories/`: IAuthRepository
- `navigation/`: AuthFeatureRoutes
- `presentation/pages/`: LoginPage, RegisterPage, ForgotPasswordPage, ChangePasswordPage

### Catalog (3 files)

- `navigation/`: CatalogFeatureRoutes
- `presentation/pages/`: CatalogScreen (design system component showcase)

### Dashboard (12 files)

- `application/`: DashboardCubit + use cases
- `data/models/`: DashboardModel
- `data/mappers/`: DashboardMapper
- `data/repositories/`: DashboardMockRepository
- `domain/entities/`: DashboardEntity
- `domain/repositories/`: IDashboardRepository
- `navigation/`: DashboardFeatureRoutes
- `presentation/pages/`: DashboardPage, DashboardHomePage

### Settings (10 files)

- `application/`: SettingsBloc + use cases
- `navigation/`: SettingsFeatureRoutes
- `presentation/pages/`: SettingsPage, SettingsScreen

### Users (24 files)

- `application/`: UserBloc, AuthorityBloc + use cases
- `data/models/`: User, Authority, UserModel
- `data/mappers/`: UserMapper
- `data/repositories/`: UserRepository, AuthorityRepository
- `domain/repositories/`: IUserRepository
- `navigation/`: UsersFeatureRoutes
- `presentation/`: UserListPage, UserEditorPage + widgets

## Infrastructure

### HTTP (infrastructure/http/)

- `http_utils.dart`: HTTP client utilities with certificate pinning support

### Configuration (infrastructure/config/)

- `environment.dart`: Environment-driven configuration for local and production modes
- `constants.dart`: Application-wide constants

### Storage (infrastructure/storage/)

- `local_storage.dart`: Local storage abstraction using shared_preferences and get_storage

## Core

### Errors (core/errors/)

- `app_api_exception.dart`: Custom API exception handling

### Logging (core/logging/)

- `app_logger.dart`: Application logging abstraction

### Security (core/security/)

- `allowed_paths.dart`: Public/private route path definitions
- `security_utils.dart`: Security utility functions

## Shared

### Design System (shared/design_system/)

14 composable components: AppButton, AppCard, AppBadge, AppAvatar, AppInput, AppSelect, AppCheckbox, AppSwitch, AppDialog, AppToast, AppSkeleton, AppDivider, AppProgressBar, AppTooltip.

4 theme files: AppTheme, AppThemePalette, SemanticColors, ThemeColors.

7 token files: AppSpacing, AppBreakpoints, AppDurations, AppSizes, AppRadius, AppElevation, AppTypography.

### Shared Widgets (shared/widgets/)

- ConfirmationDialogWidget
- ResponsiveFormWidget
- SubmitButtonWidget
- ThemeSelectionDialog
- LanguageSelectionDialog
- EditorFormMode
- UserFormFields
- WebBackButtonDisabler

## Dependency Injection

### Repository Registration (app/di/app_dependencies.dart)

All repository implementations are registered here with their interfaces.

### BLoC Registration (app/di/app_scope.dart)

All BLoCs and Cubits are provided to the widget tree from here.

## App Shell

### Responsive Layout (app/shell/)

- `app_shell.dart`: Main shell wrapper
- `responsive_scaffold.dart`: Adaptive layout for mobile/tablet/desktop
- `content_area.dart`: Main content rendering area
- `sidebar/`: Collapsible sidebar with menu items
- `top_bar/`: Top navigation bar with breadcrumb
- `bottom_nav/`: Mobile bottom navigation
- `command_palette/`: Ctrl+K command palette for quick navigation
- `drawer/`: Drawer BLoC for mobile menu

## Tech Stack

| Category | Technology |
|----------|-----------|
| Flutter | 3.41.4 |
| Dart SDK | 3.11.1 |
| State Management | flutter_bloc 9.1.1 |
| Routing | go_router 17.1.0 |
| HTTP | http 1.6.0 |
| Certificate Pinning | http_certificate_pinning 3.0.1 |
| Forms | flutter_form_builder 10.3.0+2 |
| Form Validation | form_builder_validators 11.3.0 |
| Localization | intl 0.20.2, intl_utils 2.8.14 |
| Storage | shared_preferences 2.5.4, get_storage 2.1.1 |
| Charts | fl_chart |
| PDF | pdf 3.11.3, printing 5.14.2 |
| WebView | flutter_inappwebview 6.1.5 |
| Loading Effects | shimmer |
| Image Caching | cached_network_image |
| Logging | logger 2.6.2 |
| Testing | flutter_test, bloc_test 10.0.0, mocktail |

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Flutter 3.41.4 and Dart 3.11.1
- FVM (Flutter Version Management) recommended
- Android SDK for Android builds
- Xcode for iOS and macOS builds

### Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/cevheri/flutter-bloc-advanced.git
cd flutter-bloc-advanced
fvm install 3.41.4
fvm use 3.41.4
fvm flutter pub get
```

### Run Locally With Mock Data

```bash
fvm flutter run --target lib/main/main_local.dart           # Mobile
fvm flutter run -d chrome --target lib/main/main_local.dart  # Web
```

Demo credentials: admin/admin (full access), user/user (limited access).

### Run Against Real API

```bash
fvm flutter run --target lib/main/main_prod.dart
```

### Build

```bash
fvm flutter build apk --release --target lib/main/main_prod.dart  # Android
fvm flutter build ios --target lib/main/main_prod.dart              # iOS
fvm flutter build web --target lib/main/main_prod.dart              # Web
```

### Test

```bash
fvm flutter test                    # Run all tests (472+)
fvm flutter test --coverage         # With coverage
fvm dart analyze                    # Static analysis
fvm dart format . --line-length=120 # Format code
```

## Testing Structure

472+ tests organized by feature:

```
test/
  app/           # Router, shell, DI tests
  core/          # Error, logging, security tests
  features/      # Feature-specific tests
    <feature>/
      application/     # BLoC/Cubit tests
      data/
        models/        # Model serialization tests
        repositories/  # Repository tests
      presentation/
        pages/         # Widget/page tests
        widgets/       # Component tests
      navigation/      # Route tests
  infrastructure/      # HTTP, storage tests
  shared/              # Design system, widget tests
  mocks/               # Mock classes and fake data
```

## Adding a New Feature

1. Create directory: `lib/features/<feature>/`
2. Define domain: entities in `domain/entities/`, repository interface in `domain/repositories/`
3. Implement data: models in `data/models/`, mapper in `data/mappers/`, repository in `data/repositories/`
4. Add business logic: use cases in `application/usecases/`, BLoC in `application/`
5. Build UI: pages in `presentation/pages/`, widgets in `presentation/widgets/`
6. Define routes: `navigation/<feature>_routes.dart`
7. Register DI: `app/di/app_dependencies.dart` (repos) + `app/di/app_scope.dart` (BLoCs)
8. Register routes: `app/router/app_router.dart`
9. Add translations: `l10n/` ARB files, then run `fvm dart run intl_utils:generate`
10. Write tests: `test/features/<feature>/` mirroring the feature structure

## Environments

- **Local/Dev**: Mock data from `assets/mock/*.json`, no backend required
- **Production**: Real API at configured endpoint, set in `infrastructure/config/environment.dart`
- Entry points: `main/main_local.dart` (dev), `main/main_prod.dart` (production)

## CI/CD

GitHub Actions workflows:
- `build_and_test.yml`: Build and test automation
- `build-web.yml`: Web build and GitHub Pages deployment
- `sonar_scanner.yml`: SonarQube code quality analysis
